letters_page: Future comics writer Doug Moench ("Douglas") contributes another letter, this time extolling the artwork of John Buscema, comparing it to the medium of film-making. Marvel then suggests that comics have an inherent advantage over film in that the panels are a concise condensation of all the highlights of the story's action. All the excess baggage a filmstrip must drag along with it is avoided. This allows the reader to fill in the gaps using their own imagination. Allen Greenberg laments the loss of Tales of the Watcher now that the title has gone monthly, with Marvel chiming in that they don't yet know what will become of the backup feature.
ownership: I was lucky enough to get a VF copy of this book as part of the 2000 collection, for which I paid $22, selling for $165 in Jan, 2025, netting me over $140.
